english_name: The Second Epistle of Paul the Apostle to the Corinthians

verses.latin_vulgate: Notam autem facimus vobis, fratres, gratiam Dei, quæ data est in ecclesiis Macedoniæ:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: γνωρίζομεν δὲ ὑμῖν ἀδελφοί τὴν χάριν τοῦ θεοῦ τὴν δεδομένην ἐν ταῖς ἐκκλησίαις τῆς μακεδονίας
verses.king_james: Moreover, brethren, we do you to wit of the grace of God bestowed on the churches of Macedonia;

verses.latin_vulgate: quod in multo experimento tribulationis abundantia gaudii ipsorum fuit, et altissima paupertas eorum, abundavit in divitias simplicitatis eorum:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὅτι ἐν πολλῇ δοκιμῇ θλίψεως ἡ περισσεία τῆς χαρᾶς αὐτῶν καὶ ἡ κατὰ βάθους πτωχεία αὐτῶν ἐπερίσσευσεν εἰς τὸν πλοῦτον τῆς ἁπλότητος αὐτῶν
verses.king_james: How that in a great trial of affliction the abundance of their joy and their deep poverty abounded unto the riches of their liberality.

verses.latin_vulgate: quia secundum virtutem testimonium illis reddo, et supra virtutem voluntarii fuerunt,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὅτι κατὰ δύναμιν μαρτυρῶ καὶ ὑπὲρ δύναμιν αὐθαίρετοι
verses.king_james: For to *their* power, I bear record, yea, and beyond *their* power *they were* willing of themselves;

verses.latin_vulgate: cum multa exhortatione obsecrantes nos gratiam, et communicationem ministerii, quod fit in sanctos.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μετὰ πολλῆς παρακλήσεως δεόμενοι ἡμῶν τὴν χάριν καὶ τὴν κοινωνίαν τῆς διακονίας τῆς εἰς τοὺς ἁγίους δέξασθαι ἡμᾶς
verses.king_james: Praying us with much intreaty that we would receive the gift, and *take upon us* the fellowship of the ministering to the saints.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et non sicut speravimus, sed semetipsos dederunt primum Domino, deinde nobis per voluntatem Dei,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ οὐ καθὼς ἠλπίσαμεν ἀλλ᾽ ἑαυτοὺς ἔδωκαν πρῶτον τῷ κυρίῳ καὶ ἡμῖν διὰ θελήματος θεοῦ
verses.king_james: And *this they did*, not as we hoped, but first gave their own selves to the Lord, and unto us by the will of God.

verses.latin_vulgate: ita ut rogaremus Titum, ut quemadmodum cœpit, ita et perficiat in vobis etiam gratiam istam.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἰς τὸ παρακαλέσαι ἡμᾶς τίτον ἵνα καθὼς προενήρξατο οὕτως καὶ ἐπιτελέσῃ εἰς ὑμᾶς καὶ τὴν χάριν ταύτην
verses.king_james: Insomuch that we desired Titus, that as he had begun, so he would also finish in you the same grace also.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sed sicut in omnibus abundatis fide, et sermone, et scientia, et omni sollicitudine, insuper et caritate vestra in nos, ut et in hac gratia abundetis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀλλ᾽ ὥσπερ ἐν παντὶ περισσεύετε πίστει καὶ λόγῳ καὶ γνώσει καὶ πάσῃ σπουδῇ καὶ τῇ ἐξ ὑμῶν ἐν ἡμῖν ἀγάπῃ ἵνα καὶ ἐν ταύτῃ τῇ χάριτι περισσεύητε
verses.king_james: Therefore, as ye abound in every *thing, in* faith, and utterance, and knowledge, and *in* all diligence, and *in* your love to us, *see* that ye abound in this grace also.

verses.latin_vulgate: Non quasi imperans dico: sed per aliorum sollicitudinem, etiam vestræ caritatis ingenium bonum comprobans.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὐ κατ᾽ ἐπιταγὴν λέγω ἀλλὰ διὰ τῆς ἑτέρων σπουδῆς καὶ τὸ τῆς ὑμετέρας ἀγάπης γνήσιον δοκιμάζων
verses.king_james: I speak not by commandment, but by occasion of the forwardness of others, and to prove the sincerity of your love.

verses.latin_vulgate: Scitis enim gratiam Domini nostri Jesu Christi, quoniam propter vos egenus factus est, cum esset dives, ut illius inopia vos divites essetis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: γινώσκετε γὰρ τὴν χάριν τοῦ κυρίου ἡμῶν ἰησοῦ χριστοῦ ὅτι δι᾽ ὑμᾶς ἐπτώχευσεν πλούσιος ὤν ἵνα ὑμεῖς τῇ ἐκείνου πτωχείᾳ πλουτήσητε
verses.king_james: For ye know the grace of our Lord Jesus Christ, that, though he was rich, yet for your sakes he became poor, that ye through his poverty might be rich.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et consilium in hoc do: hoc enim vobis utile est, qui non solum facere, sed et velle cœpistis ab anno priore:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ γνώμην ἐν τούτῳ δίδωμι τοῦτο γὰρ ὑμῖν συμφέρει οἵτινες οὐ μόνον τὸ ποιῆσαι ἀλλὰ καὶ τὸ θέλειν προενήρξασθε ἀπὸ πέρυσι
verses.king_james: And herein I give *my* advice: for this is expedient for you, who have begun before, not only to do, but also to be forward a year ago.

verses.latin_vulgate: nunc vero et facto perficite: ut quemadmodum promptus est animus voluntatis, ita sit et perficiendi ex eo quod habetis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: νυνὶ δὲ καὶ τὸ ποιῆσαι ἐπιτελέσατε ὅπως καθάπερ ἡ προθυμία τοῦ θέλειν οὕτως καὶ τὸ ἐπιτελέσαι ἐκ τοῦ ἔχειν
verses.king_james: Now therefore perform the doing *of it*; that as *there was* a readiness to will, so *there may be* a performance also out of that which ye have.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si enim voluntas prompta est, secundum id quod habet, accepta est, non secundum id quod non habet.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἰ γὰρ ἡ προθυμία πρόκειται καθὸ ἐὰν ἔχῃ τις εὐπρόσδεκτος οὐ καθὸ οὐκ ἔχει
verses.king_james: For if there be first a willing mind, *it is* accepted according to that a man hath, *and* not according to that he hath not.

verses.latin_vulgate: Non enim ut aliis sit remissio, vobis autem tribulatio, sed ex æqualitate.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὐ γὰρ ἵνα ἄλλοις ἄνεσις ὑμῖν δὲ θλῖψις ἀλλ᾽ ἐξ ἰσότητος ἕν τῷ νῦν καιρῷ τό ὑμῶν περίσσευμα εἴς τό ἐκείνων ὑστέρημά
verses.king_james: For *I mean* not that other men be eased, and ye burdened:

verses.latin_vulgate: In præsenti tempore vestra abundantia illorum inopiam suppleat: ut et illorum abundantia vestræ inopiæ sit supplementum, ut fiat æqualitas, sicut scriptum est:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἵνα καὶ τὸ ἐκείνων περίσσευμα γένηται εἰς τὸ ὑμῶν ὑστέρημα ὅπως γένηται ἰσότης
verses.king_james: But by an equality, *that* now at this time your abundance *may be a supply* for their want, that their abundance also may be *a supply* for your want: that there may be equality:

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui multum, non abundavit: et qui modicum, non minoravit.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καθὼς γέγραπται ὁ τὸ πολὺ οὐκ ἐπλεόνασεν καὶ ὁ τὸ ὀλίγον οὐκ ἠλαττόνησεν
verses.king_james: As it is written, He that *had gathered* much had nothing over; and he that *had gathered* little had no lack.

verses.latin_vulgate: Gratias autem Deo, qui dedit eamdem sollicitudinem pro vobis in corde Titi,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: χάρις δὲ τῷ θεῷ τῷ διδόντι τὴν αὐτὴν σπουδὴν ὑπὲρ ὑμῶν ἐν τῇ καρδίᾳ τίτου
verses.king_james: But thanks *be* to God, which put the same earnest care into the heart of Titus for you.

verses.latin_vulgate: quoniam exhortationem quidem suscepit: sed cum sollicitior esset, sua voluntate profectus est ad vos.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὅτι τὴν μὲν παράκλησιν ἐδέξατο σπουδαιότερος δὲ ὑπάρχων αὐθαίρετος ἐξῆλθεν πρὸς ὑμᾶς
verses.king_james: For indeed he accepted the exhortation; but being more forward, of his own accord he went unto you.

verses.latin_vulgate: Misimus etiam cum illo fratrem, cujus laus est in Evangelio per omnes ecclesias:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: συνεπέμψαμεν δὲ μετ᾽ αὐτοῦ τὸν ἀδελφὸν οὗ ὁ ἔπαινος ἐν τῷ εὐαγγελίῳ διὰ πασῶν τῶν ἐκκλησιῶν
verses.king_james: And we have sent with him the brother, whose praise *is* in the gospel throughout all the churches;

verses.latin_vulgate: non solum autem, sed et ordinatus est ab ecclesiis comes peregrinationis nostræ in hanc gratiam, quæ ministratur a nobis ad Domini gloriam, et destinatam voluntatem nostram:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὐ μόνον δὲ ἀλλὰ καὶ χειροτονηθεὶς ὑπὸ τῶν ἐκκλησιῶν συνέκδημος ἡμῶν σὺν τῇ χάριτι ταύτῃ τῇ διακονουμένῃ ὑφ᾽ ἡμῶν πρὸς τὴν αὐτοῦ τοῦ κυρίου δόξαν καὶ προθυμίαν ὑμῶν
verses.king_james: And not *that* only, but who was also chosen of the churches to travel with us with this grace, which is administered by us to the glory of the same Lord, and *declaration of* your ready mind:

verses.latin_vulgate: devitantes hoc, ne quis nos vituperet in hac plenitudine, quæ ministratur a nobis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: στελλόμενοι τοῦτο μή τις ἡμᾶς μωμήσηται ἐν τῇ ἁδρότητι ταύτῃ τῇ διακονουμένῃ ὑφ᾽ ἡμῶν
verses.king_james: Avoiding this, that no man should blame us in this abundance which is administered by us:

verses.latin_vulgate: Providemus enim bona non solum coram Deo, sed etiam coram hominibus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: προνοοῦμενοι καλὰ οὐ μόνον ἐνώπιον κυρίου ἀλλὰ καὶ ἐνώπιον ἀνθρώπων
verses.king_james: Providing for honest things, not only in the sight of the Lord, but also in the sight of men.

verses.latin_vulgate: Misimus autem cum illis et fratrem nostrum, quem probavimus in multis sæpe sollicitum esse: nunc autem multo sollicitiorem, confidentia multa in vos,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: συνεπέμψαμεν δὲ αὐτοῖς τὸν ἀδελφὸν ἡμῶν ὃν ἐδοκιμάσαμεν ἐν πολλοῖς πολλάκις σπουδαῖον ὄντα νυνὶ δὲ πολὺ σπουδαιότερον πεποιθήσει πολλῇ τῇ εἰς ὑμᾶς
verses.king_james: And we have sent with them our brother, whom we have oftentimes proved diligent in many things, but now much more diligent, upon the great confidence which *I have* in you.

verses.latin_vulgate: sive pro Tito, qui est socius meus, et in vos adjutor, sive fratres nostri, Apostoli ecclesiarum, gloria Christi.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἴτε ὑπὲρ τίτου κοινωνὸς ἐμὸς καὶ εἰς ὑμᾶς συνεργός εἴτε ἀδελφοὶ ἡμῶν ἀπόστολοι ἐκκλησιῶν δόξα χριστοῦ
verses.king_james: Whether *any do enquire* of Titus, *he is* my partner and fellowhelper concerning you: or our brethren be enquired of, they are the messengers of the churches, *and* the glory of Christ.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ostensionem ergo, quæ est caritatis vestræ, et nostræ gloriæ pro vobis, in illos ostendite in faciem ecclesiarum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τὴν οὖν ἔνδειξιν τῆς ἀγάπης ὑμῶν καὶ ἡμῶν καυχήσεως ὑπὲρ ὑμῶν εἰς αὐτοὺς ἐνδειξασθε καὶ εἰς πρόσωπον τῶν ἐκκλησιῶν
verses.king_james: Wherefore shew ye to them, and before the churches, the proof of your love, and of our boasting on your behalf.
